features.summary: Tesla delivered over 240,000 vehicles in Q3 2021, up 20% quarter-over-quarter and 70% year-over-year, achieving an annualized production run rate exceeding 1 million cars. Auto gross margins reached 30.5% on a GAAP basis (29% excluding regulatory credits), with operating margins approaching 15%, exceeding long-term guidance. The company generated record operating cash flows of $3.1 billion while continuing heavy investments in manufacturing, supercharging, and service capacity. Tesla is nearing first production cars at Austin and Berlin facilities, though ramp timelines remain uncertain due to new technologies and manufacturing processes.
features.management_outlook: Tesla maintains its target of 50% annual growth rate, with Q4 production dependent on parts availability. The company expects to start delivering vehicles with 4680 cells early next year, with Cybertruck production targeted for next year following current alpha testing. Austin and Berlin factories aim to produce first cars by year-end 2021, though delivery won't begin until 2022 due to regulatory requirements. Management emphasizes the challenging ramp ahead for new factories given new product and manufacturing technologies being implemented.
features.risks: Supply chain challenges and part shortages continue to prevent factories from running at full capacity, with logistics variability creating ongoing headwinds. Rising commodity costs, particularly nickel and aluminum, are impacting cost structure alongside labor shortages and expedite costs. New factory ramps at Austin and Berlin present execution risks with uncertain timelines due to new technologies and processes. Regulatory scrutiny around FSD features has intensified, with ongoing NHTSA investigations requiring careful navigation.
features.key_drivers: Strong demand growth is driving expanding backlogs and longer customer wait times, with Model Y positioned to become the world's best-selling vehicle. Shanghai factory serving as the main export hub enables supply to North America and Model Y introduction to Europe. FSD Beta program expansion to nearly 150,000 vehicles provides valuable data for autonomous driving development. Tesla Insurance launch in Texas demonstrates 30% lower collision probability for safety score users, with plans for multi-state expansion pending regulatory approvals.
